design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate maze-like levels, solve key/switch puzzles, defeat enemies and bosses, progress through episodic campaign seeking revenge
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (ammo/health) combined with environmental hazards and enemy encounters; exploration risk vs. reward for secret areas
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Caleb, an undead supernatural gunslinger seeking revenge against a demon overlord; wielding an arsenal of bizarre and horrific weapons
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Embraces graphic violence, occult horror themes, and irreverent dark humor with pop-culture references; emphasizes creative level design with environmental storytelling over narrative exposition; rewards exploration and mastery of unconventional weapon mechanics

difficulty.checkpointing: Episode-based progression with boss levels marking episode completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can discover alternate routes, secret areas with power-ups, and manage resource conservation; weapon variety allows tactical approach selection

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon arsenal unlock through progression; temporary power-ups including 'Guns Akimbo' dual-wielding mode; health and ammo pickups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Key and switch-based level progression; boss defeats unlock new episodes
replayability_hooks: Super secret areas with hidden rewards
Multiple weapon choices and secondary attack modes for tactical variety
Multiplayer modes (Bloodbath deathmatch and cooperative play)
Environmental hazards and enemy cover mechanics reward mastery

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through anachronistic level design (1920s-1940s aesthetics mixed with futuristic elements)
Occult and horror imagery establishing atmosphere
Pop-culture references and dark humor integrated into level design and enemy encounters
Backstory contained in external documentation (website/readme) rather than in-game exposition
Weird West genre mashup with supernatural elements

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses serve as episode culmination encounters; designed as significant obstacles requiring mastery of arsenal and arena navigation
notable_bosses: Tchernobog - the demon overlord, primary antagonist and final boss
Cheogh - gargoyle minion serving as progression obstacle

faction_indicators: The Cabal - occult cult with four-member leadership circle called 'The Chosen'
Tchernobog's demonic followers - various creature types
Human Cabal members serving as enemies
danger_signals: Betrayal and murder within cult hierarchy (Tchernobog kills The Chosen)
Occult rituals and dark magic practices
environmental_storytelling: Anachronistic architecture blending 1920s noir aesthetic with supernatural locations
Futuristic/retro-futuristic technology juxtaposed with period settings
